risk_if_not_implemented: Without Authorized Individuals For Hosted Assets, Applications & Services, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Control Reciprocity
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to define instances of control reciprocity within assessment boundaries.
compensating_control_1.justification: Control Reciprocity (CPL-09) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Authorized Individuals For Hosted Assets, Applications & Services (CLD-13.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Third-Party Management
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of third-party management controls.
compensating_control_2.justification: Third-Party Management (TPM-01) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Authorized Individuals For Hosted Assets, Applications & Services (CLD-13.1) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
